title: Week Ahead — Jun 15-19
headline: Binary week: CPI bombs, SpaceX mega-IPO liquidity drain, and the Fed plays referee.
thesis: We’re staring down back-to-back macro fireworks—CPI & PPI Wednesday and Thursday, then the Fed’s rate decision + dot-plot Wednesday June 17; SPY and QQQ are already breaching down below weekly EM ranges, so volatility is the base case.

Tech sector faces max stress: Oracle (ORCL) and Adobe (ADBE) print earnings on critical days, while the SpaceX IPO ($75B) Thursday is a liquidity vampire for QQQ/NVDA/MSFT—expect rebalancing flows and possible rotation into Energy (EROC IPO validates) and Defensives (XLV holds ground).

Contrarian alert: massive retail is likely chasing these mega IPOs and tech earnings, but flows are showing potential unwind—don’t chase the noise, especially day-one SpaceX (R010/R022 active); financials (XLF) and breadth deteriorating, canary in the coal mine for late cycle risk.

hot_days.reason: CPI (BMO), Oracle earnings (AMC), plus crude/oil inventories — could open box of volatility.
PPI (BMO), Adobe earnings (AMC), SpaceX IPO (AMC) — every catalyst is binary, liquidity gets sucked out.
FOMC Decision & Press Conference; Core/Retail Sales — your macro compass for summer.

rule_triggers: R006 — SPY, QQQ have closed below the daily 20 MA, that’s an exit trigger for longs.
R010/R022 — Don't chase day-one SpaceX IPO, especially after mega-raise; risk of waterfall if put walls get demolished.
R009 — XLF flirting with support, financials stressed; watch for further breakdown.
R029 — Energy IPO (EROC) after months of XLE rally + oil inventories, watch for dark pool profit-taking/consolidation.
R003/R004 — Contrarian sentiment: retail chasing, risk rising if AAII Bulls spike on tech/IPO excitement.

what_im_watching.levels: SPY 743 (weekly EM lower) // QQQ 716 (weekly EM lower) // ORCL post-earnings reaction at $587 support // ADBE post-earnings at 96.4B cap, watch implied move // SpaceX IPO day-one flow impact (watch NVDA/MSFT for weakness) // XLF at 52, close below triggers further risk-off // XLE at 59.94 (weekly EM high), EROC IPO tells if Energy rotation is exhausted

what_im_skipping: Small-cap IPOs (PBLS, FISN) and mid-cap earnings are noise this week—there’s only room for macro and mega-cap catalysts.
closing_quote: If bonds freak out or mega IPOs drain liquidity, everything breaks. Patience, react—don’t predict, and don’t chase the crowd.
